sector_rotation.explanation: Cyclicals are outperforming defensives, suggesting investors are comfortable with risk. Typically seen in early/mid expansion phases.

news_headlines.title: UnitedHealth tops quarterly estimates, hikes profit outlook as insurer manages high medical costs - CNBC
news_headlines.description: The nation's largest private insurer expects 2026 adjusted earnings of more than $18.25 per share, up from a previous outlook of greater than $17.75 per share.

news_headlines.title: 3 things to know about Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh - NPR
news_headlines.description: President Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve goes before a Senate committee today — but Kevin Warsh's confirmation could be held up by forces that are outside his control.

news_headlines.title: Meet John Ternus, the 51-year-old former swimming champ who will succeed Tim Cook as Apple CEO - Fortune
news_headlines.description: Apple just announced Tim Cook will become executive chairman of Apple’s board of directors and John Ternus will become Apple’s next CEO.

news_headlines.title: Anthropic bites back in the compute wars with Amazon partnership - Axios
news_headlines.description: Compute capacity is the currency of the AI race and is most likely to define who wins.

news_headlines.title: Airlines are about to run out of jet fuel because of the Iran war - CNN
news_headlines.description: Thought the summer travel season was looking bad? Brace yourself: It could get even worse.

news_headlines.title: Stock Market News April 20, 2026: Nasdaq snaps 13-day winning streak, Dow and S&P 500 end modestly lower as latest Iran cease-fire deadline looms - MarketWatch
news_headlines.description: Follow all the latest U.S. market action for Monday here as the war in Iran enters its eighth week

news_headlines.title: Eli Lilly’s $3.25 billion acquisition of Kelonia Therapeutics caps startup’s tortuous ride - statnews.com
news_headlines.description: Kelonia Therapeutics, a small biotech company, long struggled to stay afloat. Now it's been acquired by Eli Lilly for $3.25 billion.

news_headlines.title: Blue Origin Botched a Satellite Launch, but AST SpaceMobile Stock Is Paying the Price - Barron's
news_headlines.description: AST SpaceMobile stock is in focus after a satellite carried by a Blue Origin rocket was put into an incorrect position on Sunday.

news_headlines.title: The stock market’s record rally now faces these two big risks - MarketWatch
Supreme Court Voices Little Interest in New Curbs on SEC’s Power - Bloomberg.com
news_headlines.description: The US Supreme Court expressed little enthusiasm for curbing one of the Securities and Exchange Commission’s most important enforcement tools, as the justices weighed restricting the financial watchdog’s power to collect ill-gotten gains.

news_headlines.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud - TechCrunch
news_headlines.description: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

news_headlines.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi - TechCrunch
news_headlines.description: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

news_headlines.title: Saw a show through Ticketmaster? You may get a refund in D.C. - The Washington Post
news_headlines.description: The District reached a settlement with Live Nation, the owner of Ticketmaster, that will refund up to $8.9 million to customers.

news_headlines.title: Rivian’s factory hit by tornado ahead of R2 launch - TechCrunch
news_headlines.description: The tornado hit a newer part of the factory, primarily used for R2 logistics such as deliveries of parts. Rivian has paused operations in the building and expects to start back up sometime this week.

news_headlines.title: Exclusive | Adobe Unveils Agents for Businesses Amid Threat of AI Disruption - WSJ
news_headlines.description: The software maker’s new AI agents are aimed at getting ahead of the risk of artificial intelligence to its business

nyt_headlines.title: The Consequences of SpaceX’s Latest A.I. Moonshot
nyt_headlines.abstract: Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite internet titan may pay $60 billion for an A.I. coding start-up. It further clouds SpaceX’s once-simple business plan.

nyt_headlines.title: Lufthansa Cuts 20,000 Flights to Save Fuel as Iran War Fallout Spreads
nyt_headlines.abstract: Global jet fuel prices have jumped more than 70 percent since the start of the war, pressuring airlines in Europe, the largest consumers of jet fuel shipped through the Strait of Hormuz.

nyt_headlines.title: Oil Prices Creep Up as Investors Weigh Cease-Fire Extension
nyt_headlines.abstract: Oil prices stablized as investors scrambled to make sense of President Trump’s decision to extend the cease-fire with Iran

nyt_headlines.title: Hoarding Is Driving Energy Prices Higher Everywhere
nyt_headlines.abstract: As wealthy nations scramble to secure stocks of oil, the result is higher prices for all and shortages in vulnerable countries.

nyt_headlines.title: A $440,000 Breast Reduction: How Doctors Cashed In on a Consumer Protection Law
nyt_headlines.abstract: A law meant to end surprise medical billing accidentally created a multibillion-dollar industry that is making doctors richer.

nyt_headlines.title: Mars or the Moon or A.I.? Elon Musk’s Changing Goals for SpaceX.
nyt_headlines.abstract: As SpaceX prepares to go public, Mr. Musk has proposed moonshots that differ from the company’s original aim of reaching Mars.

nyt_headlines.title: How ‘Age Tech’ Might Help You Grow Old at Home
nyt_headlines.abstract: The budding field is turning dreams into reality for older adults who are eager to age in place, filling caregiving gaps and easing minds as America ages rapidly.

nyt_headlines.title: Two Drugs Stir Hope for Treatment of Deadly Pancreatic Cancer
nyt_headlines.abstract: In separate small clinical trials, two treatments showed promise that they could help patients with one of the most dire diagnoses in oncology.

nyt_headlines.title: Devin Nunes Departs Trump Media After 4 Years as C.E.O.
nyt_headlines.abstract: The former congressman said it was an “appropriate time” for a leadership change at Truth Social’s parent company, whose share price has floundered.

nyt_headlines.title: SpaceX Strikes Deal With Cursor for $60 Billion
nyt_headlines.abstract: The potential acquisition comes as Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite maker, which has been emphasizing artificial intelligence, is preparing to go public.

nyt_headlines.title: Fed Pick Faces Skepticism Over Claim of Independence From Trump
nyt_headlines.abstract: Kevin Warsh’s nomination to lead the Federal Reserve could still be delayed by a Justice Department investigation into the current chair, Jerome H. Powell.

nyt_headlines.title: Trump’s Pick to Lead Fed Will Face Senators at a Tricky Moment
nyt_headlines.abstract: Kevin M. Warsh has enough support to become the next chair of the Federal Reserve, but a criminal investigation into the central bank stands in his way.

nyt_headlines.title: Here’s One Way to Know if Kevin Warsh Is Up to the Job
nyt_headlines.abstract: The question of the central bank’s independence looms over the economy.

nyt_headlines.title: Trump’s Fed Pick to Defend Central Bank’s Independence on Rate Setting
nyt_headlines.abstract: Kevin M. Warsh is set to testify at the Senate Banking Committee on Tuesday even as a criminal investigation into the central bank risks delaying his ascension to become the next chair.

nyt_headlines.title: A criminal inquiry into the Fed looms large over the Warsh hearing.
